title: ulmaceae
intro: The family rank, Ulmaceae, is a taxonomic classification of plants that consists of about 40-50 species of trees and shrubs, commonly known as elms. This family is characterized by its unique leaf shapes, with simple or lobed leaves, and its ability to thrive in a wide range of environments. The Ulmaceae family is primarily found in temperate and tropical regions around the world, with the majority of its species native to Asia and Europe. Many species within this family are valued for their timber and ornamental value, making them a significant part of various ecosystems and human landscapes.
